title: User history
description: Check your understanding of how cookies, browsing history, and search history can be used to track users in this set of free practice questions designed for AP Computer Science Principles students.

assessment_items.question_content:
A user doesn't want a website to know which of the website's webpages they visit. 

**Which action(s) can the user take to prevent the website from recording their browsing history along with any form of user identifier?**

* $\text{I}$. Logging out of their account on the site
* $\text{II}$. Disabling cookies in their browser
* $\text{III}$. Restarting the browser for each page visit

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: $\text{I}$ is sufficient.
$\text{I}$ and $\text{II}$ are sufficient.
$\text{I}$, $\text{II}$, and $\text{III}$ are sufficient.
No combination of those actions is completely sufficient.

assessment_items.question_content:
**If a website wants to track which pages a user visits on their website, what is required technically?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The user must click yes on a banner displayed on the website.
The user must give the browser access to their geolocation.
The website must be served over HTTPS and use a valid digital certificate.

assessment_items.question_content:
Gael visited the website http:\/\/toast-lovers.com several times over the course of a few months. He used a browser with cookies disabled and did _not_ log in to a user account on the site.

**Based on Gael's actions, what data could the website store in its database about his visits?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

choices.content:
URL | Date | Time | Name
- | - | -
http:\/\/toast-lovers.com/avocado | 1/11/20 | 9:00 AM | Gael Bernal
http:\/\/toast-lovers.com/pbj | 2/15/20 | 10:13 AM | Gael Bernal
http:\/\/toast-lovers.com/french | 3/24/20 | 7:37 AM | Gael Bernal

URL | Date | Time | Email address
- | - | -
http:\/\/toast-lovers.com/avocado | 1/11/20 | 9:00 AM | gael@bernal-mail.com
http:\/\/toast-lovers.com/pbj | 2/15/20 | 10:13 AM | gael@bernal-mail.com
http:\/\/toast-lovers.com/french | 3/24/20 | 7:37 AM | gael@bernal-mail.com

URL | Date | Time | IP address
- | - | -
http:\/\/toast-lovers.com/avocado | 1/11/20 | 9:00 AM | 206.169.107.195
http:\/\/toast-lovers.com/pbj | 2/15/20 | 10:13 AM | 206.169.107.195
http:\/\/toast-lovers.com/french | 3/24/20 | 7:37 AM | 206.169.107.195

assessment_items.question_content:
Rolando is using a browser that's owned by a major Internet company and is concerned about the company having access to his browsing history.

**Which of these next steps would be the _least_ helpful in addressing his concerns?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Reading the browser's privacy policy to see what data they collect
Changing the browser's privacy permissions to the least permissive mode possible
Using a different browser with stricter privacy controls
Disabling cookies in the browser's privacy preferences

assessment_items.question_content:
Horacio is new to the Web and is concerned about his privacy while using a web browser.

**Which of these steps would NOT increase his privacy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Disabling auto-play of video and audio files in the browser's settings
Changing the browser's settings to disable all cookies
Changing the browser's settings to clear browsing history every day
Setting the default search engine to one that does not track search history

assessment_items.question_content:
Daxton reads the privacy policy of his browser and realizes he is uncomfortable with the browser's default privacy practices.

**What would be a good next step?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There is nothing he can do; all web browsers have the same privacy practices, per international standards.
He can switch browsers to a browser that has different privacy practices.
He can change the privacy settings in the browser to a level he is comfortable with.
He can ask their Internet Service Provider for more privacy when using that browser.

assessment_items.question_content:
A search engine collects the following search records for a user:

Query | User ID | Date | URL
- | - | -
"board games" | XE3AL08 | 01/07/20 | boardgamegeek.com
"seafood recipes" | XE3AL08 | 02/23/20 | www.seafoodwatch.org/consumers/sustainable-recipes
"news without ads" | XE3AL08 | 02/27/20 | npr.org

Each record contains four pieces of information:

1. The query typed into the search engine by the user
2. A unique ID that identifies the user
3. The date when the user typed the query
4. The URL of the first result clicked by the user

**In what way could the search engine personalize future search results based on this history?**

👁️**Note that there are 2 answers to this question.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The search engine could show an advertisement next to the search results for "Terra Mystica", a strategy board game.
If the search results includes news articles, the search engine could give higher ranking to articles from the news site NPR.
When the user searches for restaurants, the search engine could show results that are only in their local area.
The search engine could give higher ranking to search results that are more popular with the user's age group.

assessment_items.question_content:
A search engine collects the following search records for a user:

Query | User ID | Date | URL
- | - | -
"functional fitness" | A9C9415 | 09/15/19 | www.crossfit.com
"chemistry" | A9C9415 | 11/01/19 | khanacademy.org/science/chemistry
"fantasy author" | A9C9415 | 06/07/19 | www.ursulakleguin.com

Each record contains four pieces of information:

1. The query typed into the search engine by the user
2. A unique ID that identifies the user
3. The date when the user typed the query
4. The URL of the first result clicked by the user

**In what way could the search engine personalize future search results based on this history?**

👁️**Note that there are 2 answers to this question.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The search engine could show an advertisement next to the search results for "Game of Thrones", a fantasy drama TV series.
If the user searches for anything educational, the search engine could prioritize content from Khan Academy.
The search engine could customize search results for "crossfit gym" to only show gyms in the local area.
The search engine could give higher ranking to search results that are more popular with the user's gender.

assessment_items.question_content:
A search engine collects the following search records for a user:

Query | User ID | Date | URL
- | - | -
"basketball" | Y2X37AT | 01/29/19 | www.nba.com
"kobe bryant" | Y2X37AT | 12/07/18 | www.imdb.com/title/tt6794476
"video games" | Y2X37AT | 03/11/19 | www.amazon.com/Video-Games

Each record contains four pieces of information:

1. The query typed into the search engine by the user
2. A unique ID that identifies the user
3. The date when the user typed the query
4. The URL of the first result clicked by the user

**In what way could the search engine personalize future search results based on this history?**

👁️**Note that there are 2 answers to this question.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The search engine could show an advertisement next to the search results for "Pop-a-shot", an electronic basketball game.
When the user searches for any products, the search engine could prioritize search results from Amazon.
The search engine could customize search results for "basketball courts" to only show courts in the local area.
When the user searches for products, the search engine could show products that are in the price range of the user's buying preference.

assessment_items.question_content:
An advertisement company builds a profile of a user based on their browsing history across many websites and uses that profile to create more targeted advertisements.

**Which technology enables the company to aggregate the user's browsing history across multiple sites?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

choices.content: HTTP cookies
Geolocation
Encryption
Search engines

assessment_items.question_content:
Lana visits a news website she's never visited before. She notices a section called "Recommended for you" with a list of articles that are related to articles she'd been reading on other news sites.

**Which technology was most likely responsible for the recommended articles?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Web cookies
IP-based geolocation
Keylogging software
Rogue access points

assessment_items.question_content:
An analytics website offers web developers a script that can track where users are navigating and clicking on their website. The analytics script places a third-party cookie on each website that uses it.

**What records can the analytics website store about the browsing behavior of an individual?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They can record all the webpages that the individual has ever visited.
They can record all the webpages that the individual has visited that have their cookie embedded.
They can record all the webpages that the individual has clicked on in search results.
They cannot record any of the webpages visited by the user; only the website can track that.
